title: Greedy Algorithms and Exchange Arguments · Independent Algorithm Trial
summary: Allocate rooms, bandwidth, and deadlines under competing local choices. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a greedy-design decision and proof notebook with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define a locally optimal choice and the remaining subproblem precisely.
Distinguish optimal substructure from the stronger greedy-choice property.
Transform an optimal solution to include the greedy choice without worsening it.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
